# Dependency Map — Form 1040 Line 16 (Tax)

**Tax Year:** 2025
**Last updated:** 2026-06-14 (re-authored against `lines/16.md`)

Line 16 is **tax**: the regular tax on taxable income (line 15) PLUS any applicable **Box 1 (Form 8814)**, **Box 2 (Form 4972)**, and **Box 3 write-in** (5 codes per V57) add-on taxes. Per spec `lines/16.md` §2.1:

```
line16 = regularTax + Form8814_box1 + Form4972_box2 + box3_writein_total
```

`regularTax` is computed via a **7-branch decision tree** (Section 7 below). `regularTax` is preserved separately on `TaxAndCredits.regularTax` because **Form 1040 line 17 (AMT) comparison uses `regularTax`, not the total**: `line17 = max(0, tentative_AMT − regularTax)`.

Critical zero rule (spec §2.3): line 16 is NOT automatically zero when line 15 ≤ 0 — Box 1/2/3 add-ons can produce a positive line 16 with line 15 = 0.

---

## 1. Computation identity

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| Form 1040 label | Line 16 — "Tax" (with 3 checkboxes: Box 1 Form 8814, Box 2 Form 4972, Box 3 write-in) |
| Formula | `line16 = regularTax + box1Form8814 + box2Form4972 + box3Total` |
| Backend orchestrator | `computeLine16()` in `TaxReturnComputeService.java` (~line 2748) |
| Call site | `prepare()` ~line 1583 (after line 15 finalized) |
| Output container | `TaxAndCredits` on `Form1040` |
| Output type | `BigDecimal`, whole-dollar HALF_UP rounded |
| MFS protection | `computeLine16` is **18th orchestrator** in MFS-guard cascade (per 16 #1) |

---

## 2. Direct inputs

| Input | Source | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `line15TaxableIncome` | `Deductions.line15TaxableIncome` | Primary driver; null → `computeLine16` silently skips; ≤ 0 → Branch 1 ZERO |
| `filingStatus` | `filing-status` personal form | Required for bracket lookup, QDCG thresholds, FEITW status |
| `Income.qualifiedDividends` | Line 3a | Triggers QDCG worksheet when > 0 |
| `Income.capitalGainLoss` | Line 7a | Used by QDCG when Schedule D not required |
| `Income.line7bScheduleDNotRequired` | Line 7a checkbox | Flag for capital gain distributions without Schedule D |
| `ScheduleD.line15NetLongTermCapitalGainOrLoss` | Schedule D compute | QDCG / Schedule D TW input |
| `ScheduleD.line16NetCapitalGainOrLoss` | Schedule D compute | QDCG / Schedule D TW input |
| `ScheduleD.line18TwentyEightPercentRateGain` | Schedule D compute | Schedule D Tax Worksheet trigger (28% rate gain — collectibles, §1202 stock) |
| `ScheduleD.line19UnrecapturedSection1250Gain` | Schedule D compute | Schedule D Tax Worksheet trigger (depreciation recapture, 25% max) |

---

## 3. Conditional form inputs

| Form | Field(s) | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| `Form2555Taxpayer` | `line45ForeignEarnedIncomeExclusion`, `line50HousingExclusion` | **Branch 2 FEITW** controls (top priority after ZERO) |
| `Form2555Spouse` | same | Branch 2 FEITW; **MFS-guarded** at call site |
| `kiddieIncomeTaxpayer` | `hasKiddieTaxUnearnedIncome`, `childFinalTaxLine18` | **Branch 3 Form 8615** (manual entry — Form 8615 auto-compute out of scope) |
| `form8814List[*]` | `line15ExtraTax` | **Box 1** add-on (per-child summation; child gross income < $13,500) |
| `Form4972Taxpayer` | `line30Total` | **Box 2** add-on (per participant born before Jan 2, 1936; not for IRAs/403(b)s) |
| `Form4972Spouse` | `line30Total` | Box 2 add-on; **MFS-guarded** at call site |
| `16-tax-taxpayer` | `hasEcrRecapture` + `ecrAmount` | **Box 3 code ECR** — education credit recapture |
| `16-tax-taxpayer` | `hasSection962Election` + `section962TaxAmount` + `section962Form1118ForeignTaxCredits` | **Box 3 code 962** — net of Form 1118 FTC credits |
| `16-tax-taxpayer` | `hasForm8621PficTax` + `form8621Line16eAmount` | **Box 3 code 1291TAX** — Form 8621 PFIC excess distribution |
| `16-tax-taxpayer` | `hasForm8978Adjustment` + `form8978Line14Amount` | **Box 3 code Form 8978** — positive only; negative routes to Schedule 3 line 6l (advisory flag) |
| `16-tax-taxpayer` | `hasSection965iTriggered` + `section965iTriggeredAmount` | **Box 3 code 965INC** — Section 965(i) triggered deferred tax |
| Form 4952 | `line4g` | Schedule D Tax Worksheet trigger when Form 4952 line 4g > 0 even without Schedule D |

---

## 4. Outputs (TaxAndCredits — 10 fields)

| Field | Source | Consumer / PDF target |
|---|---|---|
| `tax` | `line16Total = regularTax + box1 + box2 + box3` | Form 1040 line 16 numeric box (PDF `f2_08`); line 18 = line 16 + line 17 |
| `regularTax` | `regularTax` only (no add-ons) | Form 6251 line 8; **line 17 AMT comparison uses this, not `tax`** |
| `computationMethod` | enum: `ZERO` / `FOREIGN_EARNED_INCOME` / `FORM_8615` / `SCHEDULE_D_TAX_WORKSHEET` / `QDCG` / `TAX_TABLE` / `TAX_COMPUTATION_WORKSHEET` | Logging + E2E test assertions; PDF disclosure |
| `box1Form8814Tax` | Form 8814 sum (or null if 0) | Form 1040 line 16 Box 1 |
| `box1Checked` | true when Box 1 > 0 | PDF checkbox `c2_9` |
| `box2Form4972Tax` | Form 4972 sum (or null if 0) | Form 1040 line 16 Box 2 |
| `box2Checked` | true when Box 2 > 0 | PDF checkbox `c2_10` |
| `ecrBox3Tax` + `section962Box3Tax` + `form8621Box3Tax` + `form8978Box3Tax` + `section965iBox3Tax` | Per-code amounts | (internal) |
| `box3Checked` | true when total Box 3 > 0 | PDF checkbox `c2_11` |
| `box3Code` | Comma-separated list of fired codes (per V57 multi-code support) | PDF text `f2_07` |

---

## 5. PDF fields

| CSV key | PDF field ID | Type |
|---|---|---|
| `line16_check_form8814` | `c2_9` | Checkbox — Box 1 |
| `line16_check_form4972` | `c2_10` | Checkbox — Box 2 |
| `line16_check_other_form` | `c2_11` | Checkbox — Box 3 |
| `line16_other_form_number` | `f2_07` | Text — Box 3 code (comma-separated) |
| `line16_tax` | `f2_08` | Text — total tax amount |

---

## 6. Compute order

```text
Line 15 (Deductions.line15TaxableIncome)
    ↓
computeLine16()                                ~line 2748
    1. Branch 1 (ZERO):           line15 <= 0  → regularTax = 0
    2. Branch 2 (FOREIGN_EARNED_INCOME):
        form2555Tp != null OR form2555Sp != null (MFS-guarded)
        → computeForeignEarnedIncomeTaxWorksheet(...)
    3. Branch 3 (FORM_8615):       hasKiddieTaxUnearnedIncome = true
        → childFinalTaxLine18 (manual)
        → fallback computeTaxBracket(...) + WARN log if null
    4. Branch 4 (SCHEDULE_D_TAX_WORKSHEET):
        isScheduleDTaxWorksheetRequired(scheduleD)
        → computeScheduleDTaxWorksheet(...)
    5. Branch 5 (QDCG):
        isQDCGWorksheetRequired(form1040, scheduleD)
        → computeQDCGWorksheet(...)
    6. Branches 6/7 (TAX_TABLE / TAX_COMPUTATION_WORKSHEET):
        → computeTaxBracket(line15, status)
        → method = line15 < $100k ? "TAX_TABLE" : "TAX_COMPUTATION_WORKSHEET"
    ↓
    box1 = Σ form8814List.line15ExtraTax
    box2 = (form4972Tp?.line30Total ?? 0) + (form4972Sp?.line30Total ?? 0)    (MFS-guarded)
    box3 = ecrAddon + section962Addon + form8621Addon + form8978Addon(positive only) + section965iAddon
    line16Total = regularTax + box1 + box2 + box3
    ↓
Persist on TaxAndCredits (tax + regularTax + computationMethod + box*/code)
    ↓
computeLine17()  (Form 6251 AMT — uses regularTax, NOT tax)
    ↓
line18 = line16 + line17
    ↓
computeForm1116()  (foreign tax credit — needs line16)
```

Line 16 is the **first orchestrator-based audit since 13b** — the deductions chain (12abcde → 13a → 13b → 14 → 15) is inline-computed; `computeLine16` is a dedicated orchestrator with 4 helper methods (`computeTaxBracket` + `computeQDCGWorksheet` + `computeScheduleDTaxWorksheet` + `computeForeignEarnedIncomeTaxWorksheet`) and 2 predicates (`isScheduleDTaxWorksheetRequired` ~line 3149 + `isQDCGWorksheetRequired` ~line 3174).

---

## 7. Method selection — 7-branch decision tree

**First match wins. Structurally enforced — no priority bugs possible.**

| Priority | Condition | Method | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1st | `line15 <= 0` | `ZERO` | Also gates FEITW per spec §6.3 |
| 2nd | `form2555Tp != null` OR `form2555Sp != null` (MFS-guarded) | `FOREIGN_EARNED_INCOME` | IRC §911 — top priority after ZERO |
| 3rd | `hasKiddieTaxUnearnedIncome = true` | `FORM_8615` | Manual entry of `childFinalTaxLine18`; defensive fallback to `TAX_TABLE` / `TAX_COMPUTATION_WORKSHEET` on null |
| 4th | `(SchedD.line18 > 0 OR SchedD.line19 > 0) AND SchedD.line15 > 0 AND SchedD.line16 > 0` | `SCHEDULE_D_TAX_WORKSHEET` | Handles 28% rate gain + unrecaptured §1250 gain |
| 5th | `qualDiv > 0 OR (SchedD.line15+16 both > 0) OR line7bScheduleDNotRequired` | `QDCG` | Qualified dividends and capital gain tax worksheet |
| 6th | `line15 < $100,000` (no special method) | `TAX_TABLE` | Label-only; helper produces formula value |
| 7th | `line15 >= $100,000` (no special method) | `TAX_COMPUTATION_WORKSHEET` | Label-only; same helper |

**Branches 6 and 7 share `computeTaxBracket(line15, status)`** — the `$100,000` boundary is **label-only**. The helper produces identical values for both sides of the boundary; the `computationMethod` label is the only difference, exposed for PDF disclosure and E2E test assertions.

**Schedule J is officially listed by the IRS** as a line-16 method (income averaging for farmers/fishermen) but is **out of scope** — farming/fishing income (Schedule C/F) is blocked upstream via blocking flags at the income lines. No Schedule J branch exists in `computeLine16` (per 16 #9 observation).

---

## 8. 2025 bracket constants — Tax Table / TCW unified

### Single / MFS (same through $250,525)
| Income range | Rate | Subtract |
|---|---|---|
| 0–11,925 | 10% | 0 |
| 11,925–48,475 | 12% | 238.50 |
| 48,475–103,350 | 22% | 5,086.00 |
| 103,350–197,300 | 24% | 7,153.00 |
| 197,300–250,525 | 32% | 22,937.00 |
| 250,525–626,350 (Single) / 375,800 (MFS) | 35% | 30,452.75 |
| 626,350+ (Single) | 37% | 42,979.75 |
| 375,800+ (MFS) | 37% | 37,968.75 |

### MFJ / QSS
| Income range | Rate | Subtract |
|---|---|---|
| 0–23,850 | 10% | 0 |
| 23,850–96,950 | 12% | 477.00 |
| 96,950–206,700 | 22% | 10,172.00 |
| 206,700–394,600 | 24% | 14,306.00 |
| 394,600–501,050 | 32% | 45,874.00 |
| 501,050–751,600 | 35% | 60,905.50 |
| 751,600+ | 37% | 75,937.50 |

### HOH
| Income range | Rate | Subtract |
|---|---|---|
| 0–17,000 | 10% | 0 |
| 17,000–64,850 | 12% | 340.00 |
| 64,850–103,350 | 22% | 6,825.00 |
| 103,350–197,300 | 24% | 8,892.00 |
| 197,300–250,500 | 32% | 24,676.00 |
| 250,500–626,350 | 35% | 32,191.00 |
| 626,350+ | 37% | 44,718.00 |

Brackets centralized in `ReferenceData`; encoded in `computeTaxBracket` ~line 3221.

---

## 9. 2025 QDCG thresholds

| Filing status | 0% ceiling | 20% floor |
|---|---|---|
| Single | $48,350 | $533,400 |
| MFJ / QSS | $96,700 | $600,050 |
| MFS | $48,350 | $300,025 |
| HOH | $64,750 | $566,700 |

MFS 20% floor = half of MFJ $600,050 = $300,025 per Rev. Proc. 2024-40 §3.09. Below 0% threshold → 0% on qualified dividends + LTCG; between thresholds → 15%; above 20% threshold → 20%. Ordinary income taxed at bracket rates regardless.

---

## 10. FEITW stacking formula (Branch 2)

```
ForeignEarnedIncomeTaxWorksheet.line1  = Form1040.line15
ForeignEarnedIncomeTaxWorksheet.line2a = Form 2555 line 45 (FEIE)            (taxpayer + MFJ spouse)
ForeignEarnedIncomeTaxWorksheet.line2b = Form 2555 line 50 (housing excl)    (taxpayer + MFJ spouse)
ForeignEarnedIncomeTaxWorksheet.line2c = line2a + line2b
ForeignEarnedIncomeTaxWorksheet.line3  = line1 + line2c                       (the "stack")
ForeignEarnedIncomeTaxWorksheet.line4  = tax on line3 using TCW / QDCG / SchedD
ForeignEarnedIncomeTaxWorksheet.line5  = tax on line2c using Tax Table / TCW
ForeignEarnedIncomeTaxWorksheet.line6  = max(0, line4 − line5)               ← FEITW result = regularTax
```

The stacking formula prevents tax-rate arbitrage: $100k taxable + $50k FEIE is taxed at the marginal rate that applies at $150k (high), not $100k (low).

### Known limitation — Form 2555 + Form 8615

The FEITW line 4 helper uses TCW or QDCG; the rare combination of Form 2555 + Form 8615 (child with foreign earned income exclusion) feeds TCW/QDCG into line 4 rather than Form 8615 — vanishingly rare scenario, deferred.

### Known issue — Form 2555 seed gap (observed)

Per `project_form2555_doesnt_net_wages_from_agi.md`: `foreign-earned-income-*` seeding produces Form 2555 + FEITW correctly but does **not** auto-route the exclusion through Schedule 1 line 8d. Line 1z stays at full W-2 wages; FEITW stacks on a larger base than the IRS-canonical line 15. The line-16 result is consistent with line 15 as input, but line 15 itself is higher than it should be in those scenarios.

---

## 11. Box 1 / Box 2 / Box 3 add-on details

### 11.1 Box 1 — Form 8814

```text
form8814AddonTax = Σ over form8814List of f8814.getLine15ExtraTax()
box1Checked      = form8814AddonTax > 0
```

One Form 8814 per child; child gross income must be < $13,500 (per CLAUDE.md).

### 11.2 Box 2 — Form 4972 (MFS-guarded)

```text
form4972AddonTax = (form4972Taxpayer?.line30Total ?? 0)
                 + (form4972Spouse?.line30Total ?? 0)    [null-shadowed on MFS]
box2Checked      = form4972AddonTax > 0
```

Form 4972 is per-person (one per participant born before Jan 2, 1936; not for IRAs / 403(b)s).

**MFS defensive guard** (per 16 #1): `form4972Spouse` is null-shadowed at the `computeLine16` call site (~line 1213 in `prepare()`) when `isMfsReturn=true`. Without the guard, MFS taxpayer's line 16 would wrongly include the spouse's stale Form 4972 lump-sum tax.

#### Known issue — Form 4972 Part II cap-gain double-tax

Per `project_form4972_part2_capgain_not_removed_from_line5b.md`: when Form 4972 Part II is elected on a 1099-R lump-sum distribution, the capital-gain portion is **not** removed from line 5b. Effective double-tax: cap-gain portion taxed once at the ordinary bracket via line 5b AND again at 20% via Form 4972. Per CLAUDE.md memory, this issue has been **VERIFIED CLOSED in current code per `lines/5abc.md` §9.2**.

### 11.3 Box 3 — Five write-in codes (all wired per V57)

| Code | Source field | 1040 line-16 treatment |
|---|---|---|
| `ECR` | `hasEcrRecapture` + `ecrAmount` | Add to line 16; write `ECR` |
| `962` | `hasSection962Election` + `section962TaxAmount` − `section962Form1118ForeignTaxCredits` | Add net of Form 1118 FTC credits; attach statement |
| `1291TAX` | `hasForm8621PficTax` + `form8621Line16eAmount` | Add to line 16; write `1291TAX` |
| `Form 8978` | `hasForm8978Adjustment` + `form8978Line14Amount` | Add only if **positive**; if **negative**, route to Schedule 3 line 6l (refundable credit) — see below |
| `965INC` | `hasSection965iTriggered` + `section965iTriggeredAmount` | Add to line 16; write `965INC` |

```text
box3Total = ecrAddonTax + section962AddonTax + form8621AddonTax
          + form8978AddonTax (positive only) + section965iAddonTax
box3Code  = comma-separated list of all codes that fire (per V57 multi-code support)
```

#### Form 8978 negative-amount routing

A **negative** Form 8978 line 14 amount does **NOT** go on Form 1040 line 16; it goes on **Schedule 3 line 6l** as a refundable credit. Schedule 3 line 6l is **not yet plumbed**; the backend emits a non-blocking advisory flag `LINE16_FORM_8978_NEGATIVE_ROUTES_TO_SCHEDULE3_LINE6L`. The negative amount itself is **not** added to line 16.

#### Known issue — multi-code Box 3 e2e returns 500

Per `project_line16_box3_three_codes_e2e_500.md`: **any** multi-code Box 3 (2+ codes) on `16-tax-taxpayer` returns **HTTP 500** in e2e while the equivalent Java unit test passes. Single-code combos all work. Investigation deferred; both 2-code and 3-code e2e tests are `test.skip`-ped. The compute logic is correct (Java unit tests pass); the issue is in the REST/persistence/JSON-serialization layer that e2e exercises.

---

## 12. MFS protection (18th orchestrator in cascade)

`computeLine16` is invoked once from `prepare()` (~line 1583). The call site null-shadows **two spouse-side inputs** on MFS, bundled into the 16 #1 fix:

```java
computeLine16(form1040, filingStatus, scheduleD, form8814List,
    form4972Taxpayer,
    isMfsReturn ? null : form4972Spouse,    // 16 #1: MFS guard (Box 2 leak)
    form2555Taxpayer,
    isMfsReturn ? null : form2555Spouse,    // 16 #1: MFS guard (Branch 2 FEITW leak)
    kiddieIncomeTaxpayer, line16TaxTaxpayer, flags, uid);
```

Without these guards:
- **Form 2555 spouse leak**: Branch 2 (`form2555Tp != null || form2555Sp != null`) would fire on MFS with stale spouse FEIE, stacking spouse FEIE into the FEITW bracket and OVER-stating tax.
- **Form 4972 spouse leak**: Box 2 would unconditionally add `form4972Spouse.getLine30Total()`, leaking spouse's stale lump-sum tax into MFS taxpayer's line 16.

Lock-in test: `mfsExcludesSpouseForm2555AndForm4972FromLine16` confirms post-fix `method=TAX_COMPUTATION_WORKSHEET` (not FEITW) + `box2Form4972=0` (spouse $330 NOT leaked).

`computeLine16` is the **18th orchestrator** in the MFS-guard cascade (per 16 #1).

---

## 13. Validation rules

### 13.1 Core validation

```text
if line15 is missing       → computeLine16 silently skips (gate at line 2765)
if filing_status is missing → defaults to bracket-table fallback
```

Line 16 itself emits **no blocking flags** — it gates on null line 15. Upstream blocking flags from lines 11ab / 12abcde / 13a / 13b / 14 / 15 propagate; line 16 does not add its own.

### 13.2 Method exclusivity (structural)

```text
Form 2555 priority    → Branch 2 fires before Branches 3-7 (structurally enforced)
Schedule D Tax WS     → Branch 4 fires before Branch 5 QDCG (structurally enforced)
Branch 1 ZERO         → fires before Branch 2 FEITW (handles spec §6.3 FEITW zero gate)
```

### 13.3 Form 8978 sign rule

```text
if Form8978.line14 < 0
    do NOT add to line 16; emit advisory flag LINE16_FORM_8978_NEGATIVE_ROUTES_TO_SCHEDULE3_LINE6L
    user manually claims on Schedule 3 line 6l
```

### 13.4 Zero-line15 special case

```text
if line15 <= 0:
    regularTax = 0 (Branch 1)
    BUT Box 1/2/3 add-ons may still apply
    → line16 may still be > 0
```

---

## 14. Personal forms direct to line 16

| Form ID | Person | Key fields |
|---|---|---|
| `kiddie-income-taxpayer` | Taxpayer | `hasKiddieTaxUnearnedIncome` → Branch 3 gate; `childFinalTaxLine18` → manual Form 8615 entry |
| `16-tax-taxpayer` | Taxpayer | Box 3 inputs — ECR / 962 / 1291TAX / Form 8978 / 965INC (5 codes) |
| `foreign-earned-income-taxpayer` / `-spouse` | Each | Drives Form 2555 → Branch 2 FEITW (spouse MFS-guarded) |
| `form-4972-taxpayer` / `-spouse` | Each | Per-person lump-sum distribution → Box 2 add-on (spouse MFS-guarded) |
| `form-8814-{n}` | Per child | Per-child election → Box 1 add-on |

---

## 15. Statement types affecting line 16

| Statement | Field | How used |
|---|---|---|
| `1099-DIV` | `qualifiedDividendsAmount` (box 1b) | Feeds line 3a → QDCG worksheet trigger (Branch 5) |
| `1099-R` (lump-sum) | various | Drives Form 4972 → Box 2 (when participant born before Jan 2, 1936) |
| `1099-B` / Form 8949 | various | Drives Schedule D → SchedD line 15/16/18/19 → Branches 4/5 |

Lock-in test `mfsExcludesSpouseForm2555AndForm4972FromLine16`. Backend 762 → 763. Legacy A knowledge file renamed `knowledge_line16.md` → `line-16-tax.md`. ~70-line AUDIT-TRAIL ANCHOR terminal seed at ~line 1725. FIRST ORCHESTRATOR-BASED AUDIT since 13b. |

Form 4972 Part II cap-gain double-tax VERIFIED CLOSED per `lines/5abc.md` §9.2. Multi-code Box 3 e2e 500 issue (`project_line16_box3_three_codes_e2e_500.md`) deferred — both 2-code and 3-code e2e tests `test.skip`-ped; compute logic correct (Java unit tests pass); issue in REST/persistence/JSON-serialization layer. |

---

## 17. Bottom line

For tax year 2025:
- `line16 = regularTax + Form8814_box1 + Form4972_box2 + box3_writein_total`;
- the ordinary fallback is **Tax Table under $100,000** and **Tax Computation Worksheet at $100,000 or more** (label-only boundary; same helper);
- **Form 2555, Form 8615, Schedule D Tax Worksheet, and QDCG Worksheet** can override the ordinary path (Schedule J is out of scope);
- **line 16 is NOT guaranteed to be zero just because line 15 is zero** — Box 1/2/3 add-ons can produce positive line 16 with line 15 = 0;
- `regularTax` (without add-ons) is preserved separately for the Form 1040 line 17 AMT comparison;
- multi-code Box 3 e2e tests are currently `test.skip`-ped due to a known 500 in the REST layer (compute logic is correct).
